title: Cymopterus melanotilingia
taxonomy: Cymopterus melanotilingia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the carrot family, Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cymopterus, which is a group of plants commonly known as the mountain springs parsley. The genus Cymopterus is part of the larger family Apiaceae, which includes a wide range of plants such as carrots, parsley, and fennel. Cymopterus melanotilingia is native to the southwestern United States and is often found in dry, rocky areas. Its classification also includes the subfamily Apioideae, which is a subgroup within the larger family Apiaceae.
uses: Cymopterus melanotilingia, also known as the Great Basin spring-parsley, has been traditionally used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions.
